portolimpic.note: Flat seafront run past Bogatell & Mar Bella.

sagrada.note: Book a timed slot ahead. Saimon's view tip: Cafè Sacrotel (Rosselló) or the Lamaro hotel terrace.

batllo.note: Gaudí's dragon façade on Passeig de Gràcia. Open late.

pedrera.note: Wavy stone + warrior-chimney rooftop. Two minutes from Julia's hotel.

palau.note: UNESCO concert hall — guided tours, or catch an evening concert.

santpau.note: Doménech i Montaner's pavilion city — ~10 min walk up from Sagrada.

guell.note: Book the timed monumental-zone entry. The free park areas have great views.

vicens.note: Gaudí's playful, tile-clad first house. Quieter than the others.

punxes.note: Fairytale six-spired castle (Casa Terrades). Mostly admired from outside now.

temple.note: Roman columns hidden inside a Gothic courtyard. Free.

pont.note: The lovers' arch. Go early — it's a photo magnet.

ardiaca.note: Quiet courtyard + rooftop view behind the cathedral. Free.

besada.note: The 'kiss' photo-mosaic. Best very early before the crowd.

mar.note: Pure, soaring Catalan Gothic in the heart of El Born.

pi.note: Plaça del Pi — xocolata amb xurros and a weekend artisan market.

picasso.note: His early years, chronologically. Closed Mondays — bag-check queue can be slow.

campnou.note: Football fans only. Stadium is mid-renovation — check what's open.

aquarium.note: Animal fans — the shark tunnel is the highlight. Down at Port Vell.

cosmocaixa.note: Saimon's favourite. A flooded rainforest indoors — easily half a day.

miro.note: Bright Sert building on Montjuïc with city views. Closed Mondays.

ciutadella.note: Rowing lake, the cascade fountain, and resident green parrots.

arc.note: Red-brick arch + palm promenade leading into Ciutadella.

laberint.note: Romantic neoclassical garden with a real hedge maze. Small entry fee.

grec.note: Quiet open-air theatre + gardens on Montjuïc. Free.

fountain.note: Light, water & music show below the Palau. Thu–Sun, ~21:00–22:00 in summer.

poble.note: A village of Spain's architecture — shops, workshops, sometimes concerts.

boqueria.note: Iconic but very touristy, and now partly ticketed. Closed Sundays.

santantoni.note: The local's market. Sunday morning book-and-coin market outside. Hall closed Sun.

santacaterina.note: Wavy rainbow roof, calmer than Boqueria. Closed Sundays.

bosc.note: A fairytale forest bar tucked just off La Rambla.

xampanyet.note: House cava + classic tapas at the counter. Queue early; closed Sun.

antic.note: Hidden leafy courtyard bar behind the Palau. Service is slow, the tree is worth it.

paradiso.note: Speakeasy behind a pastrami fridge — on the World's-50-Best list. Expect a queue.

cocovail.note: Craft beer + wings, relaxed and roomy.

kaelder.note: 17 taps of their own craft beer, near Santa Maria del Pi.

exotica.note: Brunch in a jungle. Open 9–16h daily; the queue builds from noon.

berry.note: Saimon's pick. Pin is approximate — drop the exact spot in via Edit.

cascantic.note: Aimless roaming through medieval lanes — bars everywhere.

born.note: Boutiques, wine bars, and Santa Maria del Mar at its heart.

raval.note: Grittier, arty, multicultural. Great street food and dive bars.

gracia.note: Village-like squares, indie shops, terraces. Lovely at dusk.

sants.note: Workaday, un-touristy neighbourhood around the big station.

montjuicamble.note: Gardens, viewpoints and museums — pair with the cable car.

bogatell.note: Easy evening swim — cleaner and calmer than Barceloneta.

marbella.note: Relaxed beach; has nudist and LGBT+ sections and two chiringuitos.

aigues.note: Flat ~10 km trail above the city. Run or cycle it for sunset.

pratcycle.note: Longer ride: Cornellà → El Prat → beach bar. Evening swim + drink. Pin approximate.

sitges.note: ~40 min by train or car. Whitewashed old town + beaches.

hotel.note: Julia's hotel — near La Pedrera, rooftop pool.
